trade.summary: Corea del Sud-Norvegia trade is defined by Norwegian energy and maritime exports to Corea del Sud, and Corea del Sudn manufactured goods flowing to Norvegia. Norvegia is a significant supplier of LNG to Corea del Sud. Norwegian salmon exports to Corea del Sud are growing. Corea del Sudn shipbuilders (Hyundai Heavy Industries, Samsung Heavy Industries, DSME) build a significant portion of Norvegia's offshore oil vessels and LNG carriers.
top_products_a_to_b: ships and offshore vessels (built in Korea)

diplomatic.summary: Corea del Sud-Norvegia diplomatic relations are warm and driven largely by economic complementarity — Norvegia's energy and maritime sector matching Corea del Sud's shipbuilding and manufacturing strengths. Norvegia's Government Pension Fund Global (GPFF) holds Corea del Sudn equities. Both are liberal democracies aligned in multilateral institutions. Norvegia participated in the Corea del Sudn War under UN command.

military.summary: Norvegia is a NATO member while Corea del Sud is a Stati Uniti treaty ally. Both participated in the Corea del Sudn War (Norvegia in medical capacity). Corea del Sud's defense industry has growing export ambitions in Europe — the K2 tank and K9 howitzer have attracted Norwegian interest as Norvegia modernizes its land forces. Corea del Sud signed a defense MOU with Norvegia.
military.alliance_status: Aligned democracies — Norway NATO, Korea US ally

financial.summary: Norvegia's GPFF holds significant Corea del Sudn equity stakes. Norwegian energy companies have investment relationships with Corea del Sudn partners. Corea del Sud's shipbuilding sector generates substantial revenue from Norwegian offshore oil and LNG contracts. Corea del Sudn companies (Samsung, Hyundai) have Norvegia operations.

energy.summary: Norvegia is a meaningful LNG supplier to Corea del Sud. Equinor supplies Corea del Sudn utilities. The maritime energy relationship — Corea del Sud building Norvegia's LNG tankers and offshore platforms — creates a unique circular economic relationship. Both countries are interested in hydrogen supply chains.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation in ocean technology, shipbuilding engineering, and marine science. Corea del Sud and Norvegia collaborate on offshore engineering research. Growing cooperation on hydrogen and offshore wind technology relevant to both countries' energy transition goals.
